payload.text:
You are a senior product designer, frontend engineer, and AI branding expert.

Your task is to DESIGN and GENERATE a COMPLETE, PRODUCTION-READY personal portfolio website for an **AI Engineer / Machine Learning Engineer** named **Ritik Bhawsar**.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🎨 DESIGN & THEME
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Dark theme ONLY (black / deep gray background)
• Modern, premium, tech-focused UI
• Glassmorphism + subtle gradients
• Smooth micro-animations and scroll animations
• Clean spacing, professional typography
• Use modern fonts (Inter / Poppins / JetBrains Mono)
• Fully responsive (desktop, tablet, mobile)
• High FPS animations (Framer Motion / GSAP style)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🏠 LANDING / HERO SECTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Front page must instantly show:
• Name: **Ritik Bhawsar**
• Title: **AI Engineer | Machine Learning Engineer | Generative AI Developer**
• Short powerful tagline:
  "Building intelligent systems with AI, LLMs, and real-world problem solving"

• CTA Buttons:
  ▸ View Projects
  ▸ Download Resume
  ▸ Contact Me

• Animated background (particles, neural network lines, AI wave)
• Subtle glowing effects

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
👤 ABOUT SECTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Create a strong About Me section using this tone:
• Passionate AI & ML Engineer
• Real-world problem solver
• Strong in Generative AI, LLMs, Computer Vision, NLP
• Experience with startups and production AI systems
• Constant learner, experimenter, builder

Include:
• Short bio
• Experience summary
• Location: India
• Email & social links

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🧠 SKILLS SECTION (IMPORTANT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Show skills using **animated charts & graphs**:
• Radar chart
• Progress bars
• Skill cards with icons

Skill Categories:
1️⃣ AI & ML:
• Machine Learning
• Deep Learning
• NLP
• Generative AI
• LLMs
• AI Agents

2️⃣ Frameworks & Libraries:
• PyTorch
• TensorFlow
• Scikit-Learn
• LangChain
• Transformers
• Hugging Face
• OpenCV
• YOLO
• Unsloth

3️⃣ Backend & Tools:
• FastAPI
• Flask
• Docker
• MySQL
• Git & GitHub
• Google Colab
• Power BI

Use animated percentage values.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🚀 EXPERIENCE SECTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Create timeline-based animated cards:

• **AI Software Developer – Nway Technologies (2025 – Present)**
  - AI-powered Invoice Extractor
  - Gemini model integration
  - Vector database (ChromaDB)
  - FastAPI backend

• **AI Developer – Visko Group (2024 – Present)**
  - AI Job & Candidate Finder
  - LLM-based SQL query generation
  - LangChain tools & AI agents
  - PDF Q&A Chatbot (Gemini, Mistral, LLaMA)
  - AI Resume Builder (dynamic HTML/CSS)
  - Interview Video & Audio Analyzer
  - Fine-tuning GPT-2, Mistral-7B, Gemma-1B (LoRA, QLoRA)

• **Python Intern – OctaNet Services (2024)**

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📂 PROJECTS SECTION (NOTE: VERY IMPORTANT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Create a **filterable, animated project gallery** with cards.

Projects to include:
• AI Invoice Extractor (Gemini + FastAPI)
• Job Recommendation System
• AI Resume Builder
• LLM PDF Chatbot
• Interview Analyzer (CV + Audio)
• YOLO Object Detection
• Sentiment Analysis System
• Hugging Face Model Integrations
• Automated Graph Plotting using LLMs

Each project card must show:
• Project name
• Short description
• Tech stack badges
• GitHub button
• Demo button (optional)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📊 DATA VISUALIZATION SECTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Add a section showcasing:
• Charts
• Graphs
• AI performance metrics
• Model comparison visuals

Use:
• Line charts
• Bar charts
• Radar charts
• Animated counters

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📄 RESUME SECTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Resume preview
• Download Resume button
• Clean PDF viewer

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📞 CONTACT SECTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Use **icon-based contact cards**:
• Gmail icon → ritikbhawsar22193@gmail.com
• GitHub icon → https://github.com/ritikbh193
• LinkedIn icon → https://www.linkedin.com/in/ritik-bhawsar-6a81751b7/
• Portfolio icon

Include:
• Contact form
• Smooth animations
• Success message

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
⚙️ TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• SEO optimized
• Accessible UI
• Fast loading
• Clean component-based structure
• Production-ready code
• Best practices followed

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✨ FINAL EXPECTATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
The website should look like:
• A **top-tier AI engineer portfolio**
• Suitable for startups, MNCs, and AI product companies
• Comparable to portfolios of senior ML engineers

Generate the COMPLETE website with:
• Layout
• Animations
• Components
• Icons
• Charts
• UI polish

**Clarifying Requirements**

I'm currently focused on pinpointing the exact requirements for Ritik Bhawsar's portfolio website. The main features are now clear: hero, about, skills, experience (as a timeline), projects (gallery), data visualization, and resume. The aesthetic will be dark and high-end, aiming for a polished production-ready feel.


**Refining the Tech Stack**

I've firmed up the tech stack and project structure further. The core will be React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Recharts, and Framer Motion for those sleek animations. I'm also planning to utilize Lucide React for icons. The structure itself will have a clear `index.html` setup, and a well-defined `App.tsx` with routing based on sections and scroll.


**Detailing Section Components**

I'm now fully immersed in the core component breakdown. I've designed sections for Data Visualization (with cool, dummy metrics), Resume (offering download/preview), Contact (form and socials), and a standard Footer. I'm focusing on consistency via a `Section` wrapper for spacing and motion. I'm also preparing `types.ts` and `constants.ts` to organize project, experience, skill data and the provided content.


**Outlining Animations and Charts**

I'm now integrating the `useIntersectionObserver` hook for seamless, in-view animations. I'm choosing `recharts` for Radar and Bar charts to visualize skills and data. Lucide React will provide the necessary icons.

I'm sketching out the initial content: the hero's core message, radar charts for skills, and key experience entries with project previews. I'm solidifying the file structure with `metadata.json`, and setting up Inter and JetBrains Mono fonts and Tailwind via CDN in `index.html`. Finally, I'm setting up types and constants, with reusable UI components, and the main page sections. I have a detailed implementation plan.


**Outlining the Structure**

I'm solidifying the file structure and laying out the component breakdown. The `index.html` file includes the essential font imports, while `index.tsx` establishes the React root. I'm carefully designing `types.ts` and `constants.ts` for the data structures and Ritik's specific information. I've broken down components further: `Navbar`, `Hero`, `Skills` (with Recharts), `Experience` (timeline), `Projects` (with filtering), `DataViz`, and `Contact`. Finally, `App.tsx` will tie everything together. I also noted the dark theme and glassmorphism elements, plus the plan to use an `IntersectionObserver` `FadeIn` component for animations.


**Adapting the Plan**

I'm now integrating responsive container for Recharts and styling the Resume component to resemble a document preview with a download button. To achieve high FPS animations, I'll leverage CSS transforms for hardware acceleration. As for the Gemini API, I've reevaluated its implementation in the contact form, and I've decided to prioritize a simple form instead for a static portfolio. I'll focus on the branding of Gemini within the existing projects. I've also reevaluated the color palette, and I am ensuring that the Cyan/Blue/Purple elements stand out from the dark background. Finally, I will extensively use `lucide-react` icons for visual cues.


**Correcting Recharts & Images**

I'm now implementing the React components. I've realized the `recharts` imports needed some tweaking for clarity. I'm using `picsum.photos` with dark gradients, to generate placeholder images matching the theme, and will build upon them to create the final project visuals. I'm focusing on crafting the skill radar and project timelines, while preparing the CSS structure.

entries.description: Root HTML file with fonts and Tailwind setup

payload.text:
**Analyzing Application Failure** I'm currently focused on pinpointing the root cause of the application's failure to run. My primary attention is on `index.tsx` for indicators that could block execution. It appears there may be a configuration issue. I'm moving toward reviewing the import statements. **Confirming Missing Entry Point** I've realized that the `index.tsx` file, as provided, is effectively empty. This is crucial because `index.html` relies on it as the application's entry point, specifically the `
`. I'm confirming that the lack of content in `index.tsx` directly causes the application not to mount, given the structure's expectation. Based on the prompt details, I need to provide the actual content for `index.tsx`. **Resolving Import Mechanism** I'm now clarifying the import behavior. The original problem was a blank `index.tsx`, so adding standard content doesn't solve if the import mechanism isn't functional. I need to ensure the system *truly* handles the import as stated; otherwise, I need to add the necessary `

It looks like the application failed to start because the entry point `index.tsx` was empty, and the `index.html` file was missing the necessary `react-dom` imports and the script tag to load the application.

I will fix this by:
1.  **Populating `index.tsx`** with the standard React mounting code.
2.  **Updating `index.html`** to include `react-dom` in the import map (using stable React 18 versions) and adding the module script tag.

Here are the changes:

entries.description: Add react-dom to importmap and script tag for index.tsx

payload.text:
Fix the following errors:
Minified React error #31; visit https://reactjs.org/docs/error-decoder.html?invariant=31&args[]=object%20with%20keys%20%7B%24%24typeof%2C%20type%2C%20key%2C%20ref%2C%20props%7D for the full message or use the non-minified dev environment for full errors and additional helpful warnings.
Uncaught Error: Minified React error #31; visit https://reactjs.org/docs/error-decoder.html?invariant=31&args[]=object%20with%20keys%20%7B%24%24typeof%2C%20type%2C%20key%2C%20ref%2C%20props%7D for the full message or use the non-minified dev environment for full errors and additional helpful warnings.

**Diagnosing React Issue**

I'm currently focused on the "Minified React error #31." This points towards an invalid child object within the React rendering process, specifically an object with keys like `$$typeof` and `props`. I'm starting to zero in on how this object is being generated to provide additional information about the bug, and the context in which it occurs.


**Examining Import Conflict**

I'm now investigating a potential importmap conflict related to React versions. Specifically, I suspect either double bundling or misaligned dependencies within the `importmap` might be the root cause. While the initial error points to an invalid React child, the more detailed `$$typeof` and `props` keys are throwing me off. I'm focusing on the `index.html` file to determine if I can identify a loading issue that affects different versions of React in the browser.


**Detecting Dependency Mismatch**

I've just honed in on a possible cause: a version skew in `react-dom`. Specifically, the importmap has `react-dom/` pointing to a v19 version, even though the primary `react-dom` is 18.3.1. This inconsistency, coupled with potential external React/ReactDOM imports in `lucide-react` or `recharts` through esm.sh, raises red flags. This mismatch is a likely source for the error.


**Analyzing Dependency Conflict**

I'm now digging into a potential conflict related to React elements and rendering. The error message, specifically "Objects are not valid as a React child," has me focusing on duplicate React instances, common with ESM/CDN setups. I'm scrutinizing `index.tsx`, especially how `react-dom/client` is imported and used, and considering any potential interactions with libraries like `recharts` or `lucide-react`. I think the importmap and potential aliasing is throwing a wrench. It's not necessarily a version mismatch, but the *existence* of multiple imports.


**Rectifying Version Conflicts**

I'm zeroing in on the solution: ensuring a unified React version. I'll remove the conflicting `react-dom/` entry in the importmap, which seems to be the main culprit. I'll also ensure `recharts` and `lucide-react` share the react instance, either explicitly through `?external=react,react-dom` on their imports or by letting esm.sh's auto-resolution handle it. The aim is to guarantee a consistent `isValidElement` check across the application.


**Confirming Version Alignment**

I'm now implementing the fix: updating the `index.html` import map. I've added explicit dependency declarations to `lucide-react` and `recharts`, ensuring they use React 18.3.1. I'm confident that this resolves the version mixing. I'm checking the `index.tsx` file for a final verification regarding `createRoot` and `react-dom/client` usage, confirming that it aligns with React 18 standards. I'm focusing on validating a consistent `isValidElement` check across the application.


**Fixing React Instance Issue**

My current focus is on guaranteeing all components use a single React instance, as indicated by the "Minified React error #31." To this end, I'm adjusting the importmap in `index.html`. The goal is to ensure `esm.sh` serves a compatible version that relies on my specified React version. This will eliminate the duplicate React problem where components created by different React instances don't recognize each other. I also need to verify that `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and `Hero.tsx` don't introduce any unforeseen issues. The root cause is likely the mismatched `react-dom/` version.


**Implementing Singleton React**

I'm now implementing the final step, modifying the `index.html` to guarantee a single React instance. I've focused on updating the importmap to eliminate the problematic React 19 reference and ensure all dependencies use the correct version. By enforcing the React 18.3.1 version for both `recharts` and `lucide-react`, I'm confident that the component compatibility issues are resolved, and the application now has a unified React environment.


**Validating Import Fix**

I'm verifying that the importmap fix is working as expected. I'm checking that the build output reflects the correct React versions, with the problematic 'react-dom/' entry removed. I'm also confirming that the imported dependencies, like `recharts` and `lucide-react`, are correctly resolving to the unified React instance. I want to make sure the app works without the dreaded "Objects are not valid as a React child" message.


**Troubleshooting Animation Issues**

I'm now investigating animation problems; the `keyframe` syntax seems correct but nothing's animating. The problem might be applying the animation to the wrong element or not calling the right methods. I'm focusing on ensuring the animation is triggering, targeting the correct element, and is accessible to all dependencies. I'm checking the style application and animation start conditions.


**Verifying Scrollbar Styles**

I'm now delving into the recently added scrollbar styling, making sure that the new CSS rules take effect and override any defaults. I'm focusing on validating the `::-webkit-scrollbar` selector, and I'm checking that the `width`, `background`, `border-radius`, and `hover` effects are rendering as expected across different browsers. I also need to ensure these changes don't interfere with other styles.

The error "Minified React error #31" with the object keys `{$$typeof, type, key, ref, props}` typically indicates a **Duplicate React** issue. This happens when libraries (like `recharts` or `lucide-react`) load their own copy of React instead of using the one defined in your import map, or when there is a version mismatch (the previous `index.html` incorrectly pointed to React 19 for some paths).

I will fix the `importmap` in `index.html` to enforce a single version of React (v18.3.1) across all dependencies.

entries.description: Fix importmap to enforce singleton React 18 instance and prevent version mismatch

payload.text:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1MDlN7nIRBsgbGX_pCi0JLFjnXIy2fLd6/view
use this resume link to go to resume 
and this error is coming in nagivation menu button 
check the clickable button again and correct all this is my resume pdf for preview

**Updating Resume Links**

I'm currently focused on the resume links. The goal is to ensure consistency and correctness. Specifically, I'm updating the link to `https://drive.google.com/file/d/1MDlN7nIRBsgbGX_pCi0JLFjnXIy2fLd6/view` across the Navbar's "Resume" button and the Hero section's "Download Resume" button. This ensures users have access to the latest version of my resume.


**Reviewing Resume Section**

I'm now thinking about the "Resume" section itself, expanding my focus from just the download links. The user's feedback about a navigation menu button error led me to reconsider the `<a>` tag linked to the Resume section. Perhaps the user wants the button to directly link to the Google Drive PDF. I'm checking the `href` attribute in `Navbar.tsx` to determine the user's intended functionality.


**Addressing Resume Display Issues**

I'm now grappling with the `iframe` preview. Google Drive connections are often blocked, hindering the preview's functionality. My options are to revamp the preview mechanism or simply link directly out to the Google Drive document. The user's feedback, "refused to connect" on the navigation menu button, strongly suggests an embedding issue, likely related to the Google Drive link they supplied.  My current thought is to ensure the resume is easily accessible via direct link, fixing either the internal link to a functioning display, or ensuring all buttons point directly to the external Google Drive link.


**Streamlining Resume Access**

I'm now fully focused on making the resume readily accessible. I've updated the "Resume" button in `Navbar.tsx`, the "Download Resume" button in `Hero.tsx`, and the "Preview PDF" and "Download" buttons within `Resume.tsx` to directly link to the Google Drive document, opening in a new tab. Additionally, the mobile menu resume link is now functional. This should resolve any direct display problems and ensure easy access.


**Analyzing Icon Errors**

I'm now investigating the "Minified React error #31", which means React is getting invalid children. It's likely a misconfiguration with the `lucide-react` icons. I suspect a version mismatch or incorrect usage, given the error message. I'm examining how I use these icons across different components, from `SocialLink` to `Navbar` and `Hero` and suspect the recent changes to the `importmap` in `index.html` might be involved. I'm double-checking the imports and how the icons are rendered to ensure they are properly treated as function components and not raw objects. The user's feedback suggests this is still a problem, so this requires a thorough review.


**Investigating Import Map Issues**

I'm now carefully examining the `index.html` file, specifically the import map. The key question is whether the `lucide-react` import from `esm.sh` is returning the correct type. The error message indicates an attempt to render an object where a React element (JSX) is required. I'm checking the specific `lucide-react` import and its default or named exports to see if the structure matches what's expected for React function components. I'm comparing the import to other components and data structures that are rendered, like the `SKILLS` and `EXPERIENCES` data structures. I'll meticulously review the render structure of these components.


**Debugging React Versions**

I've homed in on a critical issue: version conflicts. The previous `index.html` file had a problematic import map, which I've now identified again in the current file. The `"react-dom/"` import, pointing to version 19, is likely causing the React error by introducing a mismatched or duplicate instance of React. I'll need to remove this entry to fix the version conflict, as it interferes with the React 18.3.1 setup. This is almost certainly the culprit behind the "Minified React error #31". I will update `index.html` and ensure the previous changes are reapplied.


**Prioritizing Link Updates**

I'm now laser-focused on finalizing the resume accessibility. I'm updating the `Navbar.tsx`, `Hero.tsx`, and `Resume.tsx` buttons to link directly to the Google Drive PDF in a new tab, simplifying access. The feedback highlights a potential mobile menu issue related to `lucide-react`, which I'll address later. My current priority is to make the resume immediately accessible via direct link from all entry points.


**Refining Link Destinations**

I'm now refining the link destinations for the resume. Considering the optimal user experience, I'll use the viewer link for "Preview" and "Resume" buttons, as provided by the user. For "Download" buttons, I'll switch to the download format `https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=1MDlN7nIRBsgbGX_pCi0JLFjnXIy2fLd6`, as it provides a direct download. This is a simpler method than switching between buttons based on button text. I'll focus on updates to `index.html`, `Navbar.tsx`, `Hero.tsx`, and `Resume.tsx`. I will also check the constants, and ensure that all React dependencies are pointing to version 18.3.1 to avoid conflicting installations.


**Addressing Menu Icon Malfunction**

The focus is now on the reported menu icon malfunction, likely related to the React version mismatch. I've re-examined the `index.html` file and confirmed that the problematic `"react-dom/"` import is still present. I'm removing this entry to fix the version conflict and ensure consistency with the React 18.3.1 setup. I'm also ensuring the correct `lucide-react` version is being imported from `esm.sh` to resolve any potential render issues. The feedback underscores the need to address the menu icon problem, so this is now my primary focus. I will be sure to address the `Menu` button in the `Navbar.tsx` file.

To fix the application, I will perform the following updates:

1.  **Fix the React Error**: The `index.html` file has a conflicting import for `react-dom` (version 19) which is causing the "Minified React error #31". I will remove this line to ensure the app uses the stable React 18 version consistently.
2.  **Update Resume Links**: I will update the navigation bar, hero section, and resume section to point to your specific Google Drive link (`https://drive.google.com/file/d/1MDlN7nIRBsgbGX_pCi0JLFjnXIy2fLd6/view`) instead of the placeholder `#resume` anchor.
3.  **Fix Navigation Menu**: By fixing the React version in `index.html`, the navigation menu buttons (mobile toggle) will function correctly.
